channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Scammers are targeting taxpayers with this IRS text: Here's what you need to know
item.description: If you receive a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam. Criminals are impersonating the IRS to trick taxpayers into sharing personal information with them.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Attacks by Sudanese RSF paramilitaries leave hundreds dead in White Nile State
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Attacks by Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Force have killed hundreds of civilians, including infants, in White Nile state, Sudanese officials and rights groups said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Delphi murders: Judge rules against defense on key motions
item.description: A judge denied motions from Richard Allen’s attorneys seeking to reexamine his conviction in the Delphi murders case.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congratulations, March 2 is your day: A free book giveaway honors Dr. Seuss' birthday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dr. Seuss has a present for you — as long as you were born on March 2. Dr. Seuss Enterprises, which last year offered 10,000 free copies of “The Cat in the Hat” to babies born on March 2, will expand the offer this year to anyone with that birthday. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face judge who will decide whether to dismiss his criminal case
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face the federal judge on Wednesday who will decide whether to dismiss corruption charges against him after lawyers explain the abrupt change in position just weeks before an April trial.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How better water systems can help a city survive the next firestorm
item.description: Recent fires that consumed entire Los Angeles neighborhoods underscore a stark lesson -- water utilities generally aren’t designed to respond to the large urban fires growing more common as climate change worsens.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge refuses to block DOGE’s access to student borrower data
item.description: A judge is also set to rule soon on whether to block DOGE from accessing systems at seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis on Monday named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier as Florida’s attorney general, handing the state’s top law enforcement role to a trusted aide who has helped orchestrate and defend some of his most divisive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dell secures deal worth more than $5B in Elon Musk's xAI startup company
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is one step closer to becoming a reality after Dell secured a deal worth more than $5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: Illegal-smuggling coyotes now advertising at Canada border amid Trump migrant crackdown: report
item.description: Canada-based human smugglers are allegedly exploiting TikTok to advertise illegal entry into the US across the northern border amid Trump's southern crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes special appearance at NASCAR's Daytona 500 and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Suspect arrested in killing of Palm Beach County Deputy who died shielding 5-year-old daughter
item.description: A suspect has been arrested in the case of a corrections deputy who was shot and killed in south Florida last week while shielding his 5-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida sheriff says ICE partnership only the beginning in illegal migrant crackdown
item.description: St. Johns, Florida, Sheriff Robert Hardwick shared how law enforcement is working with federal immigration authorities to apprehend and enforce immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UCLA, South Carolina, Texas and Notre Dame are No. 1 seeded teams in first NCAA Tournament reveal
item.description: UCLA, South Carolina, Texas and Notre Dame would be the No. 1 seeds in the women's NCAA Tournament if it began now. The NCAA basketball selection committee on Sunday did its first reveal of the teams in line for the top 16 seeds.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: At least 9 people are dead, including 8 in Kentucky, in latest blast of winter weather
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — At least nine people have died in the most recent round of harsh weather to pummel the U.S., including eight people in Kentucky who died as creeks swelled from heavy rain and water covered roads. Kentucky Gov.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mother arrested after leaving 3 children to live alone for years
item.description: Oakland County Sheriff’s Office deputies arrested a 34-year-old Pontiac mother on Friday after she abandoned her three children for years, leaving them in horrendous living conditions.
item.dc:creator: Trevor King, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Seven more massage therapists accuse Ravens' Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: report
item.description: A total of 16 massage therapists from eight Baltimore-area spas have now shared accounts with the Baltimore Banner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Transgender man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture: police
item.description: “This is one of the most horrific crimes I've ever investigated,” Captain Kelly Swift of the New York State Police Bureau of Criminal Investigation said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trans man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture, police say
item.description: A transgender man reported missing by his family last week endured repeated acts of violence before he was found dead in a field in upstate New York, according to state police.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Emily Russell remembers feeling hopeful after she managed to get an appointment with the Federal Emergency Management Agency not long after Hurricane Helene ripped though her home in Swannanoa, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, KRON4

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KRON4

item.title: U.S. Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cher, Lady Gaga, Miley Cyrus, Snoop Dogg: 'Saturday Night Live' is feted with a concert for the ages
item.description: By the time Cher sang “If I Could Turn Back Time,” it seemed as if time had indeed been turned back, and every single “Saturday Night Live” musical guest of 50 years had magically found their way to Radio City Music Hall. Of course, it was only a smattering.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Homeowner pleads guilty to shooting Black teen Ralph Yarl who rang the wrong doorbell
item.description: LIBERTY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man pleaded guilty Friday to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, in a case that shocked the country and renewed national debate about gun policies and race in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
